[archiving selectively – mostly tweets that I wrote (not retweets) in response to nothing and without pointing to some other URL]



14 Sep 2019

A hack a day keeps the cyber incidents away



12 Sep 2019

Every software feature or piece of digital innovation brings with it a potential security vulnerability. That’s why organizational security and security-by-design in software development is vital. And a bug bounty program.



30 Aug 2019

When a data breach hits the news, are we surprised? Not really. Good news it was detected. Good news it was fixed. Wonder who’s next. - Companies don’t stand out by being breached or not. They stand out by how fast they detect and how quickly they fix.



29 Aug 2019

A bug bounty program gives you the best bang for the bug

26 Aug 2019

Hackers everywhere. HackerOne brings you the good ones and among them the best ones.



14 Aug 2019

Ihmisen olisi aina hyvä kuulua vähemmistöön ja viettää elämänsä marginaaleissa. Marginaaleista näkyy aina keskustaan, mutta päinvastaiseen suuntaan näkevät vain harvat. - Janne Saarikivi kirjassa "Suomen kieli ja mieli" (2018)



9 Aug 2019

There's no security like hacker-powered security



1 Aug 2019

Current compute power of HackerOne's hacker community: 40 quadrillion neurons



25 Jul 2019

All successful information security programs are alike, each failing one fails in its own way.



21 Jul 2019

If it ain’t broke ... it isn’t software



20 Jul 2019

Better the hack you know than the one you don't. Much much better, actually.



14 Jul 2019

Heard at a business leader gathering: Capitalism is not in a crisis (we are within acceptable standard deviations). But democracy is in a crisis.



28 Jun 2019

Ingredients of success of hub or region for tech startups: Hungry talent. Low cost of failure. High speed of adaptation. Access to huge market. Unlimited upside in case of success.



25 Jun 2019

Hackers that do not kill us make us stronger.



24 Jun 2019

First step towards diversity for a non-diverse org: Invite people from unrepresented groups to speak to your (leadership) team on topics of their expertise. Treat them as authorities in their fields. Soon you will be hiring from such groups and diversity will start to happen.



20 Jun 2019

Quoting S-1 filing: Slack’s security program consists of (i) Organizational security, (ii) Secure by design principles, and (iii) Public bug bounty program



5 Jun 2019

Each one of us is a consul of where we came from and an honorary consul of mankind. We were sent here to understand, to become understood, and to help those who have lost something to get back home to safety.



30 May 2019

Hackers!

Criminals steal stuff. Hackers help you save stuff.

Criminals leave you high and dry. Hackers will be wading into battle WITH you.

Criminals hold their knowledge and don’t share. Hackers spend weeks WITH you helping you learn.

Criminals don’t care. 

We do.

by 

@Sidragon1



29 May 2019

Schrödinger’s cat of bug bounty programs: Security team simultaneously hopes to find every vulnerability and is relieved to find none. Either way, good for security.



23 May 2019

The amazing image of a black hole produced recently depended on 100+ open source libraries produced by 21,485 code contributors. Talk about a connected & collaborative digital civilization! #GitHubSatellite



21 May 2019

Hackers help making our world more secure. Just last week HackerOne paid hackers $1,158,463 in rewards from thankful customers.



18 May 2019

Hack your cyber risk down. Get a bug bounty program.



15 May 2019

What do Santi @santi_lopezz99, Mark @BugBountyHQ and Frans @fransrosen have in common? Unlimited curiosity, unique skill, endless tenacity and a willingness to help. They also have made $1 million each on bug bounties!



14 May 2019

Humans are the weakest link of cybersecurity. And also the strongest link. Viva ethical hacking and pooled defense!



1 May 2019

HackerOne's business is about turning bad news into good news



27 Apr 2019

Trust is important for our relationship with users and organizations. Our security program consists of Organizational security, Secure by design principles, and a Public bug bounty program. – Read more in the S1 filing by Slack Technologies https://sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1764925/000162828019004786/slacks-1.htm





22 Apr 2019

The job of a CISO is to understand and assess cyber risk and then work with the rest of the business to manage the risk and bring it down to an acceptable level. (Adapted from a quote by Mike Johnson)



17 Apr 2019

Google reports 47.9M instances on the web of "Let me know what you think". We should say so about our software too: "Let me know what you think, and please report any security vulnerabilities." Seeking input and committing to improvement is the foundation of all success.



11 Apr 2019

Hackers are building a better future for us



11 Apr 2019

I won’t be perfect, but I will listen to you; I will ensure that we treat our customers, our colleagues, and our cities with respect; and I will run our business with passion, humility, and integrity. / CEO of Uber, in S-1 filing



1 Apr 2019

Ten years of tweeting. I left Sun Microsystems and MySQL at the end of March 2009. The following day I tweeted "first day of doing nothing". Since then I have tweeted 11,540 times. I like it! Hope you do too.



22 Mar 2019

Leave it to the girl scouts, and the internet will become a safe and sound place for all



14 Mar 2019

With more women in cybersecurity, cybersecurity will get better



9 Mar 2019

Organizations without a vulnerability disclosure program for their digital assets are the anti-vaxxers of the internet



5 Mar 2019

When ethical hackers find vulnerabilities in your system, it is good news. When they don’t, it is good news.



1 Mar 2019

Conundrum: Penetration tests are essential. Pentesters are among the best in the industry. Why do so many pentest reports produce no value? The current model is broken.



27 Feb 2019

Vulns. Everybody has them. Only a community can find them.



27 Jan 2019

As a CEO, I love problems because they allow us to learn and improve. I love victories because they make customers and us stronger. I love the steady state because it means we are disciplined and operationally strong.



25 Jan 2019

On HackerOne, lots of hackers find only 1 valid vulnerability in a given year. Some of those hackers are absolutely amazing, like the one who made $30,000 on just one find. Talk about a sharpshooter!



23 Jan 2019

Cyber crime is the greatest threat to every company in the world. Whitehat hackers form the greatest power to reduce cyber risk.



20 Jan 2019

"74% of business leaders say solving the cybersecurity challenges of the Internet economy will require an organized group effort." – Accenture



10 Jan 2019

It is tantamount to cybersecurity negligence not to welcome vulnerability input from the external world

24 Oct 2018

The more eyes you have on your systems the more secure they will be - Tanner Emek, hacker at #securityat



1 Oct 2018

Our goal is to be a less attractive cyber target than the rest - Ann Dunkin, CIO of the county of Santa Clara



29 Sep 2018

How can we make our digital society *inclusive*? Through transparency, collaboration, sharing of opportunity, education, protection of privacy, and with inexpensive digital access for all.



25 Sep 2018

Inclusive capitalism! We must bring *everyone* into the 4th industrial revolution. - Marc Benioff at #Dreamforce2018



18 Sep 2018

If you can't take free help then you probably can't do anything. - Jessica Wilkerson on the topic of ethical hacking



7 Sep 2018

We are Americans. We are supposed to stand up to bullies - not follow them.



29 Aug 2018

A closed security mindset leaves vulnerabilities open. An open security mindset allows you to get the vulnerabilities closed.



29 Aug 2018

Security loves badges. I received a sales email from a person who is  CEO, Chief Architect, C|CISO, CISA, CISM, CGEIT, CISSP, CSSLP, CEH, CCSK, HCISPP



24 Aug 2018

Compassionate candor: Speak the truth and hold each other accountable in a respectful, loving way



24 Aug 2018

With software deployment becoming continuous, so should security testing



15 Aug 2018

Cybersecurity happens in collaboration, not in silos. Against an asymmetric threat, pooled defense is the best defense.



14 Aug 2018

What doesn’t kill you makes you stronger. Like a hacker submitting a security vulnerability report to you. The first one may take you aback. Then you realize they are doing it for YOUR benefit.



11 Aug 2018

The more we hack you, the less hackable you become



8 Aug 2018

Let’s turn security around. It’s not about tech, it’s about people. Not secrecy, but openness. Not siloed teams, but pooled & collaborative defense. Not perimeter-based or point-in-time, but all-encompassing and on-going. Not about blame but about learning. Not slow, but fast.



7 Aug 2018

Cybersecurity is the mission-focused and risk-optimized governance of information, which maximizes confidentiality, integrity, and availability using a balanced mix of people, policy, and technology, while perennially improving over time.



1 Aug 2018

To avoid getting hacked, try to get hacked



30 Jul 2018

Too many CISOs go to their CEOs and boards with scorecards that are overladen with volumetric data on compliance challenges or technology issues. – Accenture

 .@gigastacey but getting a cloud platform production ready is hard work, and takes time. We've been at it for some time. #structurechat 

10 Oct 2012



 .@gigastacey Euca is very much ready. Running heavy production loads with web/tech/mobile companies, govt agencies. #structurechat 

10 Oct 2012



 .@gigabarb OpenStack certainly has vendor/dev popularity. CloudStack strong with service providers. Open Nebula with HPC. #structurechat 

10 Oct 2012



 .@gigabarb Open source cloud *is* the innovation. OpenStack, CloudStack, Open Nebula, Eucalyptus. Blazing the trail. #structurechat 

10 Oct 2012



 .@gigastacey We are not seeing it among our users or in the market. Other APIs may become more popular. #structurechat 

10 Oct 2012



 .@gigastacey The debate was about APIs. We believe clouds are all about APIs. Common APIs give apps freedom to run anywhere. #structurechat 

10 Oct 2012

10 Oct 2012



 .@gigastacey Yep, we do see public cloud adoption in EU - with the big but also the regional players. #structurechat

 .@gigastacey We expect privacy views to boost private and other special cloud solutions, but not seeing much of that yet. #structurechat
